chapter_title.en: Chapter 59 – Expiation of the Fast and its redemption

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/355)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/355)
text: سَأَلْتُ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ نَذَرَ نَذْراً فِي صِيَامٍ فَعَجَزَ فَقَالَ كَانَ أَبِي يَقُولُ عَلَيْهِ مَكَانَ كُلِّ يَوْمٍ مُدٌّ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Ahmad, from Musa Bin Bakr, from Muhammad Bin Mansour who said,

‘I asked (Imam) Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> about a man who vowed a vow regarding Fasts, so he was frustrated. So he<sup>asws</sup> said; ‘My<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> was saying: ‘Upon him, in place of each day, is a Mudd (approximately 750g. of food) (as charity)’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from Ali ibn Ahmad from Musa ibn Bakr from Muhammad ibn Mansur who has said the following: “I once asked al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who makes a vow and then fails to fast. He (the Imam) said, ‘My father, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would say that he is required to pay one mud (a certain measurement) for every day.’”
